title: Training Day

jokes.text: And $.10 of every dollar is going into the party fund, so we can throw parties for ourselves.
jokes.setup: Explaining the purpose of a fundraising garage sale
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of raising money to throw parties for the same people doing the fundraising
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael logic - self-defeating circular reasoning delivered with complete sincerity. Economy is excellent, character-specific worldview.

jokes.text: It's cold, dwight! / Just shut the door. Let people come in the front. / It's good that it's cold. The signs will drive business inside. The warmth will lure people inside.
jokes.setup: Cold weather making the garage sale uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Dwight's convoluted business logic that cold weather is actually strategic marketing
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight - taking a simple problem and creating an overcomplicated 'business strategy' around it

jokes.text: Schrutes are farmers by hobby, blacksmiths by trade
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he won't give away a thumbtack
jokes.punchline: Absurdly over-the-top backstory for a simple thumbtack transaction
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Dwight's grandiose self-image and bizarre family mythology in one efficient line

jokes.text: Through the art of the swap, I will walk out of here with the finest item here. I will trade you for the used candle.
jokes.setup: Dwight's grand declaration about masterful trading
jokes.punchline: His first target is a pathetic used candle
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline structure - inflated expectations immediately deflated by mundane reality

jokes.text: I love her. I, uh, I actually don't know why I brought that, because it's kind of priceless. $10. Mmm, no. 200. 500. 20. 45. Get lost. Damn it.
jokes.setup: Jim claims item is priceless and he loves it
jokes.punchline: Immediately tries to negotiate selling it, gets flustered when rejected
jokes.explanation: Nice contradiction between emotional attachment and immediate willingness to sell, escalating desperation

jokes.text: In case? In case of what? Well, you know, in case... Maybe something changes. I don't have an 'in case.' No.
jokes.setup: Pam suggests storing the neon sign 'in case'
jokes.punchline: Jim's realization that 'in case' implies their relationship might end, his defensive rejection
jokes.explanation: Excellent relationship humor - the subtext becomes text, Jim's vulnerability showing through his defensiveness. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: I am calling because I am going to have to fire your daughter holly, because she's such a terrible employee. I'm just kidding.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Holly's father with serious tone
jokes.punchline: Fake-out that he's firing her before revealing the real purpose
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate humor, but the misdirection is somewhat predictable

jokes.text: I would like to discuss my intentions with you, which are to ask her to marry me. And I was just hoping that you would give me your approval. And this isn't a joke.
jokes.setup: Michael's formal, old-fashioned request for permission
jokes.punchline: His need to clarify 'this isn't a joke' after the terrible opening
jokes.explanation: Michael's genuine sincerity undermined by his previous terrible joke, creating awkward believability issues

jokes.text: Let me tell you about men like him. He comes over, and you're like, 'hey, baby, let me light a candle,' and you pull out this one. Half used. He's like, 'who else is she seeing? I better lock her down fast.'
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to sell Dwight on buying a used candle
jokes.punchline: Bizarre psychological manipulation theory about half-used candles making men jealous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic from Kevin - completely unexpected psychological theory delivered with total confidence. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: When I was a kid, I was on dallas. Really? We missed our connecting flight, then we spent a week on hawaii.
jokes.setup: Creed claims he was on the TV show Dallas as a child
jokes.punchline: Reveals he just missed a flight and was stuck in Dallas (the city) for a week
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Creed's reality is always more mundane and weird than his initial claims. Very efficient storytelling.

jokes.text: Professor copperfield's miracle legumes? I was in jamaica, and I got lost. And it was getting dark this one night, and then out of nowhere, this guy comes with a cart, Dwight, he told me things about myself
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the origin of mysterious beans
jokes.punchline: Classic tourist scam setup with ominous 'he told me things about myself' hook
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the scam story, Jim's gullibility is funny but not quite as character-specific as it could be

jokes.text: So you want to sell me magic beans? Correction. Nice try. No, correction. Terrible try.
jokes.setup: Dwight calling out the magic beans scam
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-correction from 'nice try' to 'terrible try'
jokes.explanation: Dwight's pedantic correction of his own politeness is very character-specific and economical

jokes.text: Your friend michael, yes. What's going on? Um, I think I know, Uh, is dad there? Hollis? Hi, dad. There was a program on tv about india.
jokes.setup: Holly trying to discuss Michael's proposal call with her parents
jokes.punchline: Her father's complete non sequitur about a TV program on India
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the reality of dealing with aging parents who can't focus on important conversations

jokes.text: Well, who do you have? I have your father. He's right here. I'll put him on. No, mom. Oh, which one's decaf?
jokes.setup: Holly asking her mother who takes care of her
jokes.punchline: Mother's circular logic followed by immediate distraction about coffee
jokes.explanation: Continues the theme of aging parent confusion, the circular logic and distraction feel authentic

jokes.text: Are you proposing? Maybe. Would you light it? Do the honors, please? Yeah, no problem.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Ryan to help with his romantic fire message
jokes.punchline: Ryan casually agreeing to help with what might be a proposal without any emotional investment
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Ryan's complete lack of emotional investment in Michael's big moment

jokes.text: Michael, you've had two ideas today... And one of them was great, and the other one was terrible. I am not in the mood for riddles, pam. This is terrible. No, this is romantic.
jokes.setup: Pam trying to diplomatically tell Michael one idea is bad
jokes.punchline: Michael's impatience with her diplomacy and stubborn insistence that the fire message is romantic
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic between Pam's tactfulness and Michael's obliviousness. 'I am not in the mood for riddles' is a great character line.

jokes.text: My mom makes the best pesto in the world. And I always tell her, 'mom, you should sell this. You'd make a fortune.' And she always says, 'no, it's just for family.' Well, finally, I was like, 'f*** it, I'll sell it.'
jokes.setup: Ryan setting up a heartwarming family story about his mom's pesto
jokes.punchline: His solution is to exploit his mother's cooking without her knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - takes something wholesome and finds a way to make it selfish and exploitative. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Pesto party? Really? Anyway, she makes, like, 100 bottles' worth. It's so good. And phyllis just had that 'mom' look I wanted.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining how he tricked his mom into mass-producing pesto
jokes.punchline: His calculation that Phyllis has the right 'mom look' for his scam
jokes.explanation: Continues Ryan's sociopathic calculation - reducing people to their usefulness for his schemes. 'Mom look' is a great specific detail.

jokes.text: You got this kosher certified? No, I meant like, 'it's cool.'
jokes.setup: Customer asking about kosher certification for the pesto
jokes.punchline: Ryan's misunderstanding that 'kosher' means 'cool/acceptable'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay but works well with Ryan's character - he's too hip to understand actual kosher requirements

jokes.text: Ryan, you have such an interesting mind. So creative. All these new business ideas and artistic projects. Thank you. What you got there? Oh, it's just stanley's old photo album. I was thinking about throwing it in the garbage. I mean, why would I want some random black man's old photo album sitting on my bookshelf? I'm not james franco.
jokes.setup: Phyllis complimenting Ryan's creativity, Ryan holding Stanley's photo album
jokes.punchline: Ryan's casually racist dismissal and bizarre James Franco reference
jokes.explanation: Dark but character-appropriate humor. Ryan's casual callousness combined with the random celebrity reference is perfectly him. Risky but earned.

jokes.text: Things are going very well. I traded a thumbtack for meredith's junk, for kelly's crap, for phyllis's garbage, for oscar's trash, for stanley's crap, for ryan's junk, for creed's garbage, for a very cute squid that erin happened to have.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his trading chain progression
jokes.punchline: The culmination is 'a very cute squid that erin happened to have' - both anticlimactic and absurd
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running gag. The casual way he says 'very cute squid that erin happened to have' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I'm really sorry. All right, just out of curiosity, They're legumes, dwight. And you're just gonna make fun of me, so why would i?
jokes.setup: Jim trying to get Dwight to reveal what the beans supposedly do
jokes.punchline: Jim's defensive reaction when Dwight won't engage with his obvious scam
jokes.explanation: Jim's frustration at not being able to complete his prank is mildly funny but not exceptional

jokes.text: You called the meeting. I don't I did? Okay. Thank you all for coming. I would like to talk to you today about... Recycling.
jokes.setup: Michael apparently called a meeting but doesn't remember
jokes.punchline: His improvised topic of 'recycling' with obvious uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - improvising his way through a situation he created but doesn't remember

jokes.text: I had a great idea until you ruined it. You want to know how to do it? Take her out to dinner, go down on one knee. If you are in costume, you did something wrong.
jokes.setup: Pam giving Michael proposal advice
jokes.punchline: 'If you are in costume, you did something wrong' - universal proposal wisdom
jokes.explanation: Excellent universal truth delivered with perfect economy. Very quotable life advice disguised as comedy.

jokes.text: If at any point you find yourself tying a ring to a woodland creature, stop and look at yourself.
jokes.setup: Continuing proposal advice
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific yet universal warning about over-the-top proposals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity that feels both absurd and completely reasonable. Perfect economy and highly quotable. Could be a universal meme.

jokes.text: I'm gon' play me a little old blackmail card... Nice. And call a proxy meeting and take control of ewing oil once and for all. No, you can't do that.
jokes.setup: Kevin playing the Dallas board game
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic delivery of game moves with Southern accent
jokes.explanation: Kevin's commitment to the Dallas character voice is funny, character-appropriate enthusiasm

jokes.text: And I'm gonna play this here 'share the wealth' card. Which entitles me to half of both of you all's money. No, this card is from the wrong game. This is from the game of life. It was in the box. Well played.
jokes.setup: Kevin playing a card he claims gives him money
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be from a completely different board game, Kevin accepts this graciously
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic - Kevin's attempt to cheat backfires but he maintains dignity. The mix-up feels authentically Kevin.

jokes.text: How do you know when it's time? I don't know if you ever know. And if you wait for the day when your parent comes to you and says, 'i can't take care of myself,' it's never gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Discussion about when to move aging parents to care
jokes.punchline: The sad truth that parents will never admit they need help
jokes.explanation: Genuinely insightful observation with dark humor. Earned emotional weight makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I have a box of bras under the table if you're interested. Let me see.
jokes.setup: Meredith trying to make a sale
jokes.punchline: Offering used bras and the customer's immediate interest
jokes.explanation: Very Meredith - inappropriate but delivered matter-of-factly. The customer's interest adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: It hits the ground, the head pops off. This leads to me saying the line, 'i lost my head when I fell in love with you.' That's a guarantee. Easy enough to get a corpse.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his elaborate proposal plan involving a mannequin
jokes.punchline: 'Easy enough to get a corpse' - casual escalation to actual dead body
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mannequin to corpse delivered so casually. Perfect Michael logic - the pun justifies everything, including grave robbing.

jokes.text: A gas station? Well, it was when she was working in new york, That must have been a surprise, when, at the gas station, you proposed. No, it was really It was really sweet. It was raining and You didn't say that the weather was bad.
jokes.setup: Michael's shock at Jim proposing at a gas station
jokes.punchline: Michael's continued fixation on the location despite the romantic context
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Michael can't get past the unromantic location, missing the emotional significance

jokes.text: Get this. Kevin thought I was gonna sell my bowflex for $200, and I told him, 'dude, this was a prop in my movie.'
jokes.setup: Ryan bragging about his bowflex sale
jokes.punchline: Claiming it was a movie prop to justify higher price
jokes.explanation: Classic Ryan - lying to inflate the value of mundane items. 'My movie' is a nice touch of grandiosity.

jokes.text: My dad isn't doing so well. Oh, okay. For how long? I don't know. And I want you to come with me. Now, I Okay. And I was thinking, you know, and that we're both ready... Michael scott, will you No, no. shh!
jokes.setup: Holly's emotional moment about her father, leading to her starting to propose
jokes.punchline: Michael shushing her because he wants to propose first
jokes.explanation: Great reversal - Holly beats him to the proposal but Michael's ego can't handle not being in control of the moment

jokes.text: Uh... What? No, I am not going to be proposed to in the break room. That is not going to be our story.
jokes.setup: Holly realizing where she almost proposed
jokes.punchline: Her horror at the unromantic location and concern about 'their story'
jokes.explanation: Relatable horror at unromantic proposal location. 'That is not going to be our story' is very quotable.

jokes.text: Should have burned this place down when I had the chance.
jokes.setup: Michael's frustration at the proposal being ruined
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to arson as a solution to workplace problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - extreme overreaction delivered deadpan. The casual nature makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And that is dallas. Nice. Not bad, right? I can't really tell. I know, right? What the Oh, my god. That It is, right? I mean, it's impossible.
jokes.setup: End of the Dallas board game
jokes.punchline: Everyone's amazed reactions to something we can't see but is apparently impossible/amazing
jokes.explanation: Mystery reaction beat - we don't see what happened but their amazement suggests something impossible occurred

jokes.text: All right, I'll take 'em. They're probably worthless. Probably. Leave the telescope.
jokes.setup: Creed buying Jim's magic beans
jokes.punchline: Both acknowledge they're probably worthless but Creed wants them anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed logic - acknowledges they're worthless but wants them anyway. Good callback resolution.

jokes.text: I started with a thumbtack and ended up with a telescope. But in a way, the most valuable thing here wasn't the telescope at all. No. It was this packet of beans, because they're magic.
jokes.setup: Dwight's philosophical reflection on his successful trading
jokes.punchline: Concluding that the worthless magic beans were the most valuable item
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Dwight's rationalization and genuine belief in magic. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: How'd we do? $13. That's great.
jokes.setup: Asking about total garage sale earnings
jokes.punchline: Pathetically low total of $13, delivered as if it's a success
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor - the enthusiasm for such a small amount is perfectly deadpan

jokes.text: So this is one of my favorite places in the world. why? This is where toby announced It was the happiest day of my life... Until the day you came to replace him.
jokes.setup: Michael showing Holly a meaningful location
jokes.punchline: It's meaningful because it's where Toby announced he was leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael character moment - making a romantic gesture about his hatred of Toby. Great callback to series-long running gag.

jokes.text: And this is where we first made love. Michael!
jokes.setup: Michael continuing his romantic tour of the office
jokes.punchline: Claiming they made love in the office, Holly's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate oversharing in a romantic context, Holly's reality check

jokes.text: When I called you to tell you that I had herpes, and that I was still in love with you. And you said that it was over And thank goodness It was an ingrown hair. Yes.
jokes.setup: Michael recounting their phone conversation about herpes
jokes.punchline: The relief that it was just an ingrown hair
jokes.explanation: Callback to previous awkward Michael moment, the medical misunderstanding is character-appropriate

jokes.text: This is where we coran our first meeting. Remember? Obesity awareness? Saved a lot of lives that day. That's where you first met michael klump. Oh, I sayI say I sit on you.
jokes.setup: Michael recounting their first meeting during obesity seminar
jokes.punchline: Reference to Michael Klump character and the inappropriate threat
jokes.explanation: Deep callback to specific character and episode, Michael's commitment to the bit in a romantic context

jokes.text: Holly, will you marry me? no. Marry me, holly. no. That guy's got more than he can handle as it is. Will you marry me? no. That marriage would be a sham.
jokes.setup: Michael practicing proposals on various coworkers
jokes.punchline: Each person's specific reason for rejecting Michael's proposal
jokes.explanation: Good escalating gag with character-specific responses. Each rejection has its own logic and humor.

jokes.text: It started with a broken
jokes.setup: Michael attempting to create letters in the parking lot
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of poorly formed letters spelling out proposal
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Michael's plan going wrong, typical Michael execution failure

jokes.text: Holly flax... Marrying me will you be? Your wife becoming... Be will i.
jokes.setup: Michael's actual proposal attempt
jokes.punchline: His Yoda-like backwards speech pattern during the most important moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Michael's nervousness causes him to speak like Yoda during his proposal. Highly quotable and perfectly Michael.

jokes.text: Holly has to go back to colorado. I'm going with her. I'm leaving.
jokes.setup: Michael announcing the engagement news
jokes.punchline: Immediately following with the news that he's leaving, deflating the celebration
jokes.explanation: Good emotional whiplash - from celebration to loss. Michael's timing creates perfect dramatic irony.

tmdb_overview: The new manager, Deangelo Vickers, has everyone hoping to make good first impressions: Andy finds himself awkwardly typecast while Jim and Pam worry that they've come on too strong. Only Dwight is apathetic about the new leader.
